Title: Cisgenic overexpression of cytosolic glutamine synthetase improves nitrogen utilization efficiency in barley and prevents grain protein decline under elevated CO2

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: GenBank: JX878489; cDNA probe KF815944; EMBL accession numbers KF815944, KF815945
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Ammonium assimilation into glutamine during N remobilization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent incorporation of ammonium into glutamate to form glutamine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and supports N remobilization required for grain protein accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in N assimilation/remobilization; central but not explicitly established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed nutrient reserve and determinant of grain nutritional quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cisgenic overexpression increased GS1 activity, improved grain yield, nitrogen utilization efficiency and nitrogen harvest index under low and high N, and prevented decline of grain protein concentration under elevated CO2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cisgenic overexpression of native genomic HvGS1-1; qRT-PCR; GS enzyme activity assays after FPLC separation of GS1/GS2; Western blot confirmation of isoforms; greenhouse phenotyping under multiple N and CO2 regimes; grain and tissue N/protein measurements
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low, high and excessive nitrogen supply; ambient versus elevated atmospheric CO2 (800-900 ppm)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Under ambient CO2 and low/high N, cisgenic lines often had lower grain protein concentration than wild type despite higher yield; excessive N abolished yield advantage and was relatively more detrimental to cisgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in leaves during reproductive growth and senescence-associated N remobilization; cisgene included native promoter and terminator
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In cisgenic lines, 2-3-fold higher expression in the two youngest fully developed leaves at 28 DAG; elevated GS1 activity in upper leaves at 49, 70 and 91 DAG during reproductive growth
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase family, GS1 cytosolic isoform
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising cisgenic target to improve barley NUE and grain yield under low to high N and to stabilize grain protein under elevated CO2; deployment should avoid excessive N and preserve native regulation to reduce pleiotropic penalties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Introducing an extra native HvGS1-1 copy increased HvGS1-1 transcript abundance and GS1 enzyme activity about twofold. 2. HvGS1-1 overexpression increased grain yield, NUE and N harvest index under low and high N without increasing total shoot N. 3. Under elevated CO2, HvGS1-1 overexpression prevented the decline in grain protein concentration seen in wild type. 4. Benefits were lost under excessive N, indicating sensitivity to C/N balance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GS2 activity also increased transiently in upper leaves before and after ear emergence, suggesting coordinated regulation between cytosolic and chloroplastic GS isoforms; the transformed lines were described as not fully developed cisgenic lines because marker/vector backbone presence was not excluded
